factors.summary: Labor relations are stable as negotiations have begun and agreements are in place.

evidence.text: Separately, in January 2015 we reached an agreement covering wages and work rules through 2019 with the Brotherhood of Locomotive Engineers and Trainmen (BLET) which represents approximately 4,600 of our locomotive engineers. Changes to the BLET benefit plan will be bargained nationally through the NCCC.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Stable labor relations with agreements in place suggest a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are a significant factor with potential to affect financial performance.

evidence.text: Prolonged negative changes in domestic and global economic conditions affecting the producers and consumers of the commodities we carry may have an adverse effect on our financial position, results of operations, or liquidity in a particular year or quarter.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Potential adverse effects from economic conditions pose a risk, indicating negative sentiment.

factors.summary: Interest rate fluctuations could impact financial results, though considered unlikely to be material.

evidence.text: We consider it unlikely that interest rate fluctuations applicable to these instruments will result in a material adverse effect on our financial position, results of operations, or liquidity.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Unlikely material impact from interest rate fluctuations suggests a neutral sentiment.

factors.summary: Talent retention is manageable as the company is in a stable position regarding workforce.

evidence.text: More than 80% of our railroad employees are covered by collective bargaining agreements with various labor unions. These agreements remain in effect until changed pursuant to the Railway Labor Act.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Stable position with collective bargaining agreements indicates positive sentiment for talent retention.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable with some fluctuations in pay rates and benefits.

evidence.text: Compensation and benefits decreased $105 million, or 3%, in 2014, reflecting changes in postretirement and pension benefit costs, health and welfare benefit costs, pay rates, and payroll taxes.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Manageable labor costs with some fluctuations are neither clearly positive nor negative, leading to a neutral sentiment.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is stable with slight decreases in 2014 but still sufficient to meet obligations.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities was $2.9 billion in 2014 and $3.1 billion in both 2013 and 2012.
evidence.subsection: FINANCIAL CONDITION, LIQUIDITY, AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Stable free cash flow despite slight decreases shows a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: For 2015, we have budgeted $2.4 billion for property additions.
evidence.subsection: FINANCIAL CONDITION, LIQUIDITY, AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capital expenditures signal confidence in future growth, indicating a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving with a decrease in the debt-to-total capitalization ratio.

evidence.text: Our debt-to-total capitalization ratio was 42.1% at December 31, 2014, compared with 45.6% at December 31, 2013.
evidence.subsection: FINANCIAL CONDITION, LIQUIDITY, AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement in debt-to-total capitalization ratio shows positive sentiment towards debt leverage.

factors.summary: Litigation exposure is manageable with ongoing audits and no material expected impact.

evidence.text: The IRS examinations have been completed for all years prior to 2011. We do not expect that the resolution of the examination will have a material effect on our financial position, results of operations, or liquidity.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Litigation exposure is manageable with no expected material impact.

factors.summary: Environmental compliance is a significant cost with ongoing expenses and property additions.

evidence.text: Operating expenses for environmental matters totaled $45 million in 2014, $57 million in 2013, and $40 million in 2012.
evidence.subsection: Personal Injury, Environmental, and Legal Liabilities

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Ongoing significant costs and expenses suggest a negative impact.

factors.summary: Overall demand is stable as higher volumes in some segments offset declines in others.

evidence.text: Strong volume growth in our intermodal and merchandise businesses driven by higher demand for rail services offset declines in our coal business, resulting in a $379 million, or 3%, improvement in our operating revenues.
evidence.subsection: Norfolk Southern Corporation and Subsidiaries / OVERVIEW

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Stable demand with growth in key segments indicates positive trends.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is improving, driven by volume increases in key segments.

evidence.text: Strong volume growth in our intermodal and merchandise businesses driven by higher demand for rail services offset declines in our coal business, resulting in a $379 million, or 3%, improvement in our operating revenues.
evidence.subsection: Norfolk Southern Corporation and Subsidiaries / OVERVIEW

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving organic growth driven by volume increases.

factors.summary: Pricing power is stable, with some segments experiencing lower average revenue per unit due to rate changes and mix effects.

evidence.text: The increase in 2014 resulted from higher volumes, partially offset by lower average revenue per unit as the effects of lower rates and changes in the mix of business more than offset higher fuel surcharges.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Stable pricing power with mixed effects from rate changes and mix.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, particularly in coal, which is negatively impacting revenue.

evidence.text: Coal revenues decrease d $161 million , or 6% , compared with 2013 , reflecting a 5% decrease in carload volume. Average revenue per unit was down 2% , the result of lower pricing (mainly market-based export coal) and the negative effect of changes in mix.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards lower-margin segments negatively impacts revenue.

factors.summary: Forward guidance expects continued improvement in 2015, with revenue growth offsetting potential fuel surcharge declines.

evidence.text: Looking forward to 2015, we expect the impact of lower oil prices on our fuel surcharge revenue programs to be offset by increased volumes and higher rates.
evidence.subsection: Norfolk Southern Corporation and Subsidiaries / OVERVIEW

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expectation of continued improvement with revenue growth offsetting declines.

factors.summary: The intermodal and merchandise segments are growing fastest, while coal and export markets are slowing.

evidence.text: Strong volume growth in our intermodal and merchandise businesses driven by higher demand for rail services offset declines in our coal business, resulting in a $379 million, or 3%, improvement in our operating revenues.
evidence.subsection: Norfolk Southern Corporation and Subsidiaries / OVERVIEW

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growth in key segments despite slowing in others shows positive momentum.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by increases in locomotive and equipment maintenance and repair costs as well as higher demand in intermodal and merchandise segments.

evidence.text: Volume growth in 2014 and 2013 drove increases in locomotive and equipment maintenance and repair costs for both years.

Strong volume growth in our intermodal and merchandise businesses driven by higher demand for rail services offset declines in our coal business, resulting in a $379 million, or 3%, improvement in our operating revenues.
evidence.subsection: Norfolk Southern Corporation and Subsidiaries / OVERVIEW

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates unit volumes are growing, driven by increases in maintenance and repair costs as well as higher demand in key segments, which suggests positive momentum.

factors.summary: The operating margins are showing a positive trajectory, driven by both cost control measures and favorable tax adjustments, which have contributed to an improved financial performance.

evidence.text: The 2014 and 2013 years benefited from favorable reductions in deferred tax expense for state law changes and certain business tax credits.

Our continued focus on cost control helped stem growth in operating expenses (up $61 million, or 1%) despite higher volumes and led to an all-time low operating ratio (a measure of the amount of operating revenues consumed by operating expenses) of 69.2% for 2014.
evidence.subsection: Norfolk Southern Corporation and Subsidiaries / OVERVIEW

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Operating margins are improving due to cost control and favorable tax adjustments, indicating strong financial performance.

factors.summary: The company experienced mixed trends in input costs, with fuel prices decreasing in 2014 but increasing in 2013, impacting operating expenses. Additionally, fuel surcharge revenue, which is a significant driver of input costs, is expected to decline due to lower oil prices.

evidence.text: The decrease in 2014 was principally the result of lower locomotive fuel prices (down 6%), offset in part by higher locomotive fuel consumption (up 4%).

Over 85% of our revenue base is covered by negotiated fuel surcharges, and revenues in 2014 included $1,329 million of such surcharges. This amount was divided about evenly between surcharges tied to the monthly average price of West Texas Intermediate Crude Oil (WTI) and surcharges tied to the weekly average price of On-Highway Diesel (OHD).

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed trends in input costs with both decreases and increases in different years, along with expected declines in fuel surcharge revenue, suggest no clear directional trend.

factors.summary: Cost reduction actions are partially offsetting increases in expenses.

evidence.text: Increases in volume-related costs were offset in part by lower postretirement and pension benefit costs as well as lower fuel prices.

Our continued focus on cost control helped stem growth in operating expenses (up $61 million, or 1%) despite higher volumes and led to an all-time low operating ratio (a measure of the amount of operating revenues consumed by operating expenses) of 69.2% for 2014.
evidence.subsection: Norfolk Southern Corporation and Subsidiaries / OVERVIEW

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction actions are offsetting expense increases, showing effective management and contributing to financial improvement.

factors.summary: Fuel surcharge revenue is highly sensitive to oil prices, which could significantly impact near-term results.

evidence.text: For every one half percent change in the 2014 effective tax rate, net income would have changed by $16 million.

Over 85% of our revenue base is covered by negotiated fuel surcharges, and revenues in 2014 included $1,329 million of such surcharges. This amount was divided about evenly between surcharges tied to the monthly average price of West Texas Intermediate Crude Oil (WTI) and surcharges tied to the weekly average price of On-Highway Diesel (OHD).

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: High sensitivity to oil prices, which could significantly impact near-term results, poses a notable risk and headwind.
